reasoning: Without access to real-time daily proxy data for October 2025 (e.g., ERA5) due to tool limitations, I must rely on a base rate. The question asks about a narrow 0.05°C bin (1.10°C to 1.14°C) for the GISTEMP anomaly. Such narrow bins generally carry around a 10-25% probability a priori if they are close to the expected mean. Given the cooling impact of a widely expected La Niña by late 2025 following the 2023-2024 El Niño spikes, this bin is highly plausible as a central outcome. However, without current observational data from the month itself, it is impossible to be highly confident in any specific 0.05°C band. Therefore, I assign a base rate probability of 15%.
